Your AI Files Analyst
6/20/2026, 5:27:04 PM
I need help developing UI interfaces for a program I am developing. The first page needs to be a dashboard. A simple launch point for all the apps that the program will support. This is for a science fiction project so it should have a futuristic feel, darker colors with gold text.
gpt-5.5

Futuristic Dashboard UI (Dark + Gold) — First Page Concept

Below is a simple, “command terminal” style dashboard that matches your requirements: dark theme, gold text, futuristic sci‑fi feel, and acts as a launch point for all apps/modules.

1) HTML (Dashboard Page)

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Nexus Command Terminal</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <!-- Top Bar -->  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Galactic Compendium</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <!-- Main Content Grid -->  
    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Left: Quick Launch -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Navigation</h2>  

        <div class="launch-buttons" role="navigation" aria-label="App launcher">  
          <button class="tile" data-app="atlas">  
            <span class="tile-icon">🌌</span>  
            <span class="tile-title">Atlas</span>  
            <span class="tile-sub">Star maps & nav overlays</span>  
          </button>  

          <button class="tile" data-app="compendium">  
            <span class="tile-icon">📖</span>  
            <span class="tile-title">Galactic Compendium</span>  
            <span class="tile-sub">Lore & histories</span>  
          </button>  

          <button class="tile" data-app="generators">  
            <span class="tile-icon">⚙</span>  
            <span class="tile-title">Generators</span>  
            <span class="tile-sub">Run modules & create outputs</span>  
          </button>  

          <button class="tile" data-app="log">  
            <span class="tile-icon">📋</span>  
            <span class="tile-title">System Log</span>  
            <span class="tile-sub">Events, errors, audit trail</span>  
          </button>  
        </div>  

        <div class="panel-footer">  
          <div class="hint">Select a module to launch.</div>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Right: Live Console -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console">  
          <div class="console-line">[EHGC] System initialized. Waiting for operations...</div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="clearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="runSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <!-- Footer -->  
    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Nexus Command Terminal • v0.1</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app.js"></script>  
</body>  
</html>

2) CSS (Dark Futuristic + Gold)

/* styles.css */  

:root{  
  --bg0: #04060b;  
  --bg1: #070a12;  
  --panel: rgba(12, 16, 26, 0.7);  
  --panel2: rgba(10, 14, 24, 0.5);  
  --gold: #d7b86a;  
  --gold2:#f1dc9a;  
  --muted:#93a2b8;  
  --border: rgba(215, 184, 106, 0.25);  
  --shadow: rgba(0,0,0,0.55);  
  --glow: rgba(215,184,106,0.35);  
}  

*{ box-sizing:border-box; }  
html, body { height: 100%; }  
body{  
  margin:0;  
  font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, sans-serif;  
  color: var(--gold2);  
  background:  
    radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 20% 10%, rgba(215,184,106,0.12), transparent 60%),  
    radial-gradient(900px 600px at 70% 20%, rgba(215,184,106,0.08), transparent 55%),  
    linear-gradient(180deg, var(--bg0), var(--bg1));  
}  

.app{  
  max-width: 1200px;  
  margin: 0 auto;  
  padding: 18px;  
}  

.topbar{  
  display:flex;  
  align-items:center;  
  justify-content:space-between;  
  gap: 14px;  
  padding: 14px 16px;  
  border: 1px solid var(--border);  
  border-radius: 14px;  
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(10,14,24,0.85), rgba(7,10,18,0.65));  
  box-shadow: 0 16px 40px var(--shadow);  
  position: sticky;  
  top: 14px;  
  backdrop-filter: blur(10px);  
}  

.brand{  
  display:flex;  
  align-items:center;  
  gap: 12px;  
}  

.brand-badge{  
  width: 42px;  
  height: 42px;  
  border-radius: 12px;  
  border: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.35);  
  background:  
    radial-gradient(circle at 30% 25%, rgba(215,184,106,0.35), transparent 45%),  
    linear-gradient(145deg, rgba(215,184,106,0.15), rgba(0,0,0,0));  
  box-shadow: 0 0 22px var(--glow);  
}  

.brand-title{  
  font-weight: 900;  
  letter-spacing: 0.12em;  
  color: var(--gold2);  
  line-height:1.0;  
  font-size: 20px;  
}  

.brand-subtitle{  
  color: var(--muted);  
  font-size: 12px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;  
  margin-top: 3px;  
  text-transform: uppercase;  
}  

.topbar-right{  
  display:flex;  
  align-items:flex-end;  
  gap: 18px;  
  flex-wrap: wrap;  
  justify-content: flex-end;  
}  

.status{  
  display:flex;  
  align-items:center;  
  gap: 10px;  
  color: var(--gold);  
  font-weight: 800;  
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;  
  font-size: 12px;  
  text-transform: uppercase;  
}  

.dot{  
  width: 10px;  
  height: 10px;  
  border-radius: 999px;  
  background: #35d07f;  
  box-shadow: 0 0 16px rgba(53,208,127,0.45);  
}  

.clock{  
  color: var(--muted);  
  font-size: 12px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;  
}  

.grid{  
  display:grid;  
  grid-template-columns: 0.95fr 1.05fr;  
  gap: 14px;  
  margin-top: 14px;  
}  

@media (max-width: 980px){  
  .grid{ grid-template-columns: 1fr; }  
  .topbar{ position: relative; top: auto; }  
}  

.panel{  
  border: 1px solid var(--border);  
  border-radius: 16px;  
  background: var(--panel);  
  box-shadow: 0 18px 50px rgba(0,0,0,0.35);  
  padding: 14px;  
}  

.panel-title{  
  margin: 4px 0 12px;  
  font-size: 12px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.18em;  
  text-transform: uppercase;  
  color: var(--gold);  
}  

.launch-buttons{  
  display:grid;  
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;  
  gap: 12px;  
}  

@media (max-width: 520px){  
  .launch-buttons{ grid-template-columns: 1fr; }  
}  

.tile{  
  text-align:left;  
  border-radius: 14px;  
  border: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.25);  
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(10,14,24,0.7), rgba(10,14,24,0.35));  
  padding: 12px;  
  cursor:pointer;  
  color: var(--gold2);  
  transition: transform 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease;  
  position: relative;  
  overflow:hidden;  
}  

.tile::after{  
  content:"";  
  position:absolute;  
  inset:-40px;  
  background: radial-gradient(circle at 30% 20%, rgba(215,184,106,0.18), transparent 50%);  
  transform: translateY(20px);  
  opacity: 0;  
  transition: opacity 120ms ease;  
}  

.tile:hover{  
  transform: translateY(-2px);  
  border-color: rgba(215,184,106,0.45);  
  box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(215,184,106,0.12);  
}  

.tile:hover::after{  
  opacity: 1;  
}  

.tile-icon{ display:block; font-size: 22px; margin-bottom: 6px; }  
.tile-title{ display:block; font-weight: 850; letter-spacing: 0.02em; }  
.tile-sub{  
  display:block;  
  margin-top: 6px;  
  color: var(--muted);  
  font-size: 12px;  
  line-height: 1.25;  
}  

.panel-footer{  
  margin-top: 14px;  
  padding-top: 12px;  
  border-top: 1px dashed rgba(215,184,106,0.25);  
}  

.hint{  
  color: var(--muted);  
  font-size: 12px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;  
}  

.console-meta{  
  display:flex;  
  gap: 12px;  
  flex-wrap: wrap;  
  margin-bottom: 12px;  
}  

.meta-item{  
  flex: 1;  
  min-width: 160px;  
  padding: 10px;  
  border-radius: 14px;  
  background: var(--panel2);  
  border: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.18);  
}  

.meta-label{  
  color: var(--muted);  
  font-size: 12px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;  
  text-transform: uppercase;  
}  

.meta-value{  
  margin-top: 6px;  
  font-size: 20px;  
  font-weight: 900;  
  color: var(--gold2);  
}  

.console{  
  background: #05070d;  
  border-radius: 14px;  
  border: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.18);  
  padding: 12px;  
  height: 220px;  
  overflow:auto;  
  box-shadow: inset 0 0 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.6);  
}  

.console-line{  
  color: var(--gold2);  
  font-size: 13px;  
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;  
  line-height: 1.45;  
  padding: 6px 0;  
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.08);  
}  

.console-actions{  
  display:flex;  
  gap: 10px;  
  margin-top: 12px;  
}  

.btn{  
  flex:1;  
  border-radius: 14px;  
  border: 1px solid rgba(215,184,106,0.25);  
  background: rgba(10,14,24,0.45);  
  color: var(--gold2);  
  padding: 10px 12px;  
  cursor:pointer;  
  font-weight: 800;  
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;  
}  

.btn.primary{  
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(215,184,106,0.22), rgba(215,184,106,0.08));  
  border-color: rgba(215,184,106,0.45);  
  box-shadow: 0 0 26px rgba(215,184,106,0.12);  
}  

.footer{  
  margin-top: 14px;  
  padding: 12px 6px 4px;  
  text-align:center;  
  color: rgba(147,162,184,0.85);  
  font-size: 12px;  
}

3) JavaScript (Clock + Launch Routing)

// app.js  

function formatUTC(date) {  
  const hh = String(date.getUTCHours()).padStart(2, "0");  
  const mm = String(date.getUTCMinutes()).padStart(2, "0");  
  const ss = String(date.getUTCSeconds()).padStart(2, "0");  
  return `${hh}:${mm}:${ss} UTC`;  
}  

const utcClock = document.getElementById("utcClock");  
setInterval(() => {  
  utcClock.textContent = formatUTC(new Date());  
}, 250);  

utcClock.textContent = formatUTC(new Date());  

// Placeholder counters (wire to your backend later)  
document.getElementById("activePrograms").textContent = "14";  
document.getElementById("outputQueue").textContent = "0";  

// Launch tiles  
document.querySelectorAll(".tile").forEach(btn => {  
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {  
    const app = btn.dataset.app;  

    // Simple approach: route by changing location hash.  
    // Later you can swap this for real router navigation.  
    window.location.hash = `#/app/${app}`;  
    appendLog(`[EHGC] Launch request: ${app}`);  
  });  
});  

const consoleEl = document.querySelector(".console");  
function appendLog(line) {  
  const div = document.createElement("div");  
  div.className = "console-line";  
  div.textContent = line;  
  consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
  consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
}  

// Buttons  
document.getElementById("clearConsole").addEventListener("click", () => {  
  consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  
  appendLog("[EHGC] Console cleared.");  
});  

document.getElementById("runSelfTest").addEventListener("click", () => {  
  appendLog("[EHGC] Running self-test...");  
  setTimeout(() => appendLog("[EHGC] Self-test complete: OK"), 800);  
});

4) Next Step (Quick Question)

To tailor this to your project, what’s your intended front-end navigation approach?

  1. Multi-page (separate HTML files per app/module)
  2. Single-page app with hash routing (like #/app/atlas)
  3. Embedded panes (tabs/side panels) where “Atlas” and “Compendium” appear inside the dashboard page

Tell me which one you want, and I’ll adapt the dashboard to generate clean routes and panel containers for each module.

1
gpt-5.5

What I can tell from your provided sources (and what I can’t)

Your context includes excerpts from Traveller 5 Core Rules (v5.09) and a small excerpt from Mongoose Traveller 2e 2022 Update—but your query is just:

1

That doesn’t specify what question you want answered (e.g., UI element “1”, app “1”, a requirement list item, or a rule number).

Please clarify

What does “1” refer to? For example:

  1. Do you mean UI section number 1 (Dashboard “1”)?
  2. Do you want help implementing the dashboard launch tiles (Tile #1)?
  3. Do you mean Traveller rule number 1 from one of the books (which table/page/section)?
  4. Something else entirely?

Reply with one sentence like: “For item 1, I need …” and (if applicable) tell me the app/page/feature name.

Once you clarify, I’ll respond in Markdown and, where relevant, tie the design choice back to the Traveller content you provided (using your chunk citations format).

Multi-page (separate HTML files per app/module)
gpt-5.5

Multi-page (separate HTML files per app/module) UI approach

Given your decision for #1 Multi-page, the cleanest structure is:

  • index.html = Dashboard / Nexus Command Terminal
  • One HTML file per module (app):
    • atlas.html
    • generators.html
    • compendium.html
    • log.html
    • settings.html (optional)
  • Use consistent styling across all pages (styles.css)
  • Use shared UI/console behaviors via a shared JS file (app-ui.js) if you want the console/log feel consistent

Your dashboard currently has tiles for multiple modules and an output console, so routing from dashboard tiles should be simple link/navigation (no hash routing needed).

1) Recommended folder layout

/your-app/  
  index.html  
  atlas.html  
  compendium.html  
  generators.html  
  log.html  
  settings.html  
  styles.css  
  app.js                 (dashboard-specific, if you want)  
  app-ui.js              (shared helpers: theming, console logging, clock)  
  /assets/  
    icons/  
    fonts/              (optional)

2) Dashboard tile navigation (index.html)

Replace the window.location.hash approach with normal navigation to the module HTML pages.

In your dashboard JS, change click handling like this:

// app.js (dashboard)  

const tileMap = {  
  atlas: "atlas.html",  
  generators: "generators.html",  
  compendium: "compendium.html",  
  log: "log.html",  
  // settings can be added similarly  
};  

document.querySelectorAll(".tile").forEach(btn => {  
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {  
    const app = btn.dataset.app;  
    const url = tileMap[app];  

    if (url) window.location.href = url;  
  });  
});

This matches the “launch point” concept: tiles become real module entrypoints.

3) Shared futuristic look (styles.css)

Keep all theme styling in styles.css (like the dark background + gold typography you already drafted). Then each module page just links:

<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />

4) A consistent top bar + console pattern across module pages

For a “command terminal” feel, each module page should include:

  • the same top bar branding + system status/clock
  • a left content panel (module UI)
  • a right console/log panel (optional, but strongly matches the dashboard)

Example module skeleton (e.g., atlas.html):

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Atlas</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  
<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Atlas Operations</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Atlas</h2>  
        <!-- Atlas UI goes here -->  
        <p style="color: var(--muted); font-size: 13px; margin-top: 8px;">  
          Star maps, sector overlays, and navigation data will render here.  
        </p>  
      </section>  

      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  
        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="console">  
          <div class="console-line">[EHGC] Atlas interface initialized.</div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="clearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="runSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Nexus Command Terminal • Atlas</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    // page-specific init can go here  
    // e.g., populate atlas widgets, load last selection, etc.  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

5) Passing state between pages (important with multi-page)

Multi-page navigation makes it harder to keep UI state automatically. Common options:

  1. URL parameters
    • e.g. atlas.html?system=25Orionis
  2. localStorage (simple and effective for “last selected system”)
    • dashboard sets localStorage.setItem("lastSystem", ...)
    • module reads it on load
  3. SQLite/backend (you’ll likely do this later anyway)
    • module queries “what was last generated” for a given session/user

For your sci-fi console feel, localStorage is a fast win.

Example (dashboard on tile click):

// before navigating  
localStorage.setItem("lastLaunch", "atlas");  
window.location.href = "atlas.html";

Then in atlas.html:

const last = localStorage.getItem("lastLaunch");

Quick question (so I generate the right next files)

Do you want the modules to share the same “Console” panel on every page (like in the dashboard), or only show the console on the dashboard and have modules be “main-work only”?

only show the console on the dashboard and have modules be “main-work only
gpt-5.5

Yes—make modules “main-work only” and keep the Console only on the dashboard

Based on your goal (dashboard has the launch point + a single shared console), the UI pattern you want maps nicely to Traveller’s “Console hierarchy” idea: the Console is a crew interface for interaction/monitoring, while the modules are the task work behind the panels (your Generator/Atlas/Compendium pages) and don’t need to show the console UI everywhere.

UI rule of the system

  • Dashboard (index.html): shows the Console panel (system log, active programs, output queue).
  • Modules (atlas.html, generators.html, compendium.html, etc.): show only the module’s main interface.
  • Module pages send “events” to the dashboard console (later via backend; for now via localStorage or a lightweight message endpoint).

This matches the distinction between:

  • Consoles as user-facing control/feedback interfaces
  • Mainframes/computers as background task resolvers that drive console outputs

1) Dashboard: keep Console + receive module events

In your current dashboard HTML, keep the Console section exactly where it is:

  • active programs
  • output queue
  • console log output

Add a simple “event receiver” in app.js:

// app.js (dashboard)  
// Poll for module events placed into localStorage  
function readEventFromStorage() {  
  const raw = localStorage.getItem("ehgc_event");  
  if (!raw) return null;  

  localStorage.removeItem("ehgc_event");  
  return JSON.parse(raw);  
}  

setInterval(() => {  
  const evt = readEventFromStorage();  
  if (!evt) return;  

  appendLog(`[${evt.module}] ${evt.message}`);  
  // TODO: update activePrograms/outputQueue based on evt.type  
}, 300);  

function appendLog(line) {  
  const consoleEl = document.querySelector(".console");  
  const div = document.createElement("div");  
  div.className = "console-line";  
  div.textContent = line;  
  consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
  consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
}

2) Module pages: remove the Console panel entirely

For example, atlas.html should include only:

  • top bar (optional, for consistency)
  • an Atlas main panel area
  • no right-side console panel

Minimal module skeleton:

<!-- atlas.html -->  
<main class="panel">  
  <h2 class="panel-title">Atlas</h2>  
  <!-- Atlas controls/render area -->  
  <div style="color: var(--muted); font-size: 13px;">  
    Star maps, sector overlays, and celestial navigation data render here.  
  </div>  
</main>

No console markup in module pages.

3) Module pages: “write events” to dashboard

When the user clicks Generate / runs a step / changes selection on a module page, write an event for the dashboard to pick up.

Example in generators.html:

function sendDashboardEvent(module, message, type = "log") {  
  localStorage.setItem("ehgc_event", JSON.stringify({ module, message, type, at: Date.now() }));  
}

Use it:

document.getElementById("generateBtn").addEventListener("click", () => {  
  sendDashboardEvent("GEN", "Generate requested.", "enqueue");  
  // later: after backend completes  
  // sendDashboardEvent("GEN", "Generation complete.", "complete");  
});

4) Backend-friendly later upgrade (optional now, planned later)

When you’re ready to integrate Python + SQLite:

  • modules call your backend endpoint (e.g., /api/generate)
  • dashboard polls a shared queue/log table (SQLite) or uses SSE/WebSocket later

This keeps the “Console only on dashboard” model while still reflecting “consoles provide feedback governed direction; background systems resolve tasks”.

Quick confirmation question

Do you want the dashboard to automatically return to the dashboard when a module starts generating (so the user immediately sees console updates), or should it stay where the user is on the module page?

I've changed my mind I want consoles on every generator page
gpt-5.5

Change of plan: show a Console on every generator page

That’s totally workable—and it actually maps well to how Traveller frames interfaces:

  • A Console is the crew-facing UI that provides feedback-governed direction to mechanisms.
  • It handles routine processes automatically, while exceptions require user input.

So for your UI: each generator page can have its own Console panel (local feedback + status), even though the “real” global log/routing can still be optional.

Note: Traveller describes consoles as interfaces that monitor and adjust mechanisms, while higher-level exceptions come back to the user for decisions.

Recommended page template (per generator module)

For every module page (atlas.html, generators.html, compendium.html, log.html, etc.) use the same layout:

  • Left / main: module work area
  • Right: Console panel (active programs, output queue, scrollback log, buttons)

This keeps the UX consistent and futuristic.

Example: atlas.html skeleton with Console included

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Atlas</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  
<body>  
  <div class="app">  

    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Atlas Operations</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Main work only -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Atlas</h2>  

        <div class="panel-body">  
          <!-- Atlas UI widgets go here -->  
          <p style="color: var(--muted); font-size: 13px; margin-top: 8px;">  
            Star maps, sector overlays, and celestial navigation data will render here.  
          </p>  

          <button class="btn primary" id="atlasGenerate">⚡ GENERATE</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Console ON every page -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="console">  
          <div class="console-line">[EHGC] Atlas interface initialized.</div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="clearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="runSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Nexus Command Terminal • Atlas</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script src="module-console.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    // Module page-specific event wiring  
    document.getElementById("atlasGenerate").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      moduleConsole.enqueue("ATLAS", "Generate requested…");  
      // later: call backend; on completion:  
      // moduleConsole.complete("ATLAS", "Generation complete.");  
    });  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

Shared JS: one console system, reused everywhere

Create module-console.js so every page can:

  • append log lines
  • update “Active Programs” / “Output Queue”
  • clear console
  • (optionally) forward events to the dashboard later
// module-console.js  
window.moduleConsole = (function () {  
  const consoleEl = document.getElementById("console");  
  const activeEl = document.getElementById("activePrograms");  
  const queueEl = document.getElementById("outputQueue");  

  function log(line) {  
    const div = document.createElement("div");  
    div.className = "console-line";  
    div.textContent = line;  
    consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
    consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
  }  

  let active = Number(activeEl?.textContent || "0");  
  let queue = Number(queueEl?.textContent || "0");  

  function setCounters() {  
    if (activeEl) activeEl.textContent = String(active);  
    if (queueEl) queueEl.textContent = String(queue);  
  }  

  function enqueue(module, message) {  
    queue += 1;  
    setCounters();  
    log(`[${module}] ${message}`);  
  }  

  function complete(module, message) {  
    if (queue > 0) queue -= 1;  
    active = Math.max(0, active); // adjust if you track active programs  
    setCounters();  
    log(`[${module}] ${message}`);  
  }  

  function clear() {  
    if (consoleEl) consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  
    active = 0;  
    queue = 0;  
    setCounters();  
    log("[EHGC] Console cleared.");  
  }  

  // Buttons (only if present)  
  const clearBtn = document.getElementById("clearConsole");  
  clearBtn?.addEventListener("click", clear);  

  const selfTestBtn = document.getElementById("runSelfTest");  
  selfTestBtn?.addEventListener("click", () => {  
    log("[EHGC] Running self-test…");  
    setTimeout(() => log("[EHGC] Self-test complete: OK"), 800);  
  });  

  // Initial counter sync  
  setCounters();  
  return { enqueue, complete, log, clear };  
})();

Should the dashboard console still exist?

You can do either:

  1. Dashboard console only for global routing, while module consoles are local-only.
  2. Both dashboard and module consoles: module consoles also forward events to the dashboard (using localStorage or later SQLite/backend).

If you want both, tell me and I’ll provide the event-forwarding pattern.

Quick question

Do you want each module console to:

  • (A) start “fresh” on page load (local-only history), or
  • (B) share history with the dashboard/global log (so users see one continuous timeline across pages)?
Yes dashboard console should still exist. star "fresh" on page load (local-only history). Each one should have a unqiue boot sequence. main dashboard console would log app access and track results. If it was sector survey it would record top level data Sector: Number of systems generated, etc...
gpt-5.5

Plan: Consoles on every Generator page + Dashboard Console (global log)

Yes—keep the Dashboard Console. Each Generator page also gets its own local (fresh) console history that resets on page load, while the Dashboard Console provides the global timeline and app/result tracking.

This matches Traveller’s idea that consoles provide feedback-governed direction and handle routine monitoring without being the user’s higher-level brain each moment.

1) UI behavior rules

A) Main Dashboard Console (global)

  • History persists only within the current dashboard session (or you can keep it longer later).
  • Logs:
    • app/module access (e.g., “Atlas opened”, “Generators launched”)
    • completion results (e.g., “Sector survey finished”)
    • counts / top-level summary fields your backend returns

Example top-level entries:

  • APP_ACCESS atlas.html
  • JOB_START atlas:sector_survey
  • JOB_RESULT sector:systems=24 worlds=3 starports=2

B) Each Generator Console (local-only history, “fresh” per load)

  • On page load, each module’s console:
    • starts empty (or shows only a “console initialized” boot line)
    • does not share scrollback with dashboard
  • Each module has a unique boot sequence (visual + timed lines)

This reflects consoles as task enablers that resolve routine processes and provide user feedback, while “exceptions” are the user’s decision layer.

2) Implementation pattern (no backend yet)

A) Shared event contract (frontend-only)

When a generator page runs something, it emits a structured “event” to the dashboard via localStorage (simple + works with multi-page):

Event payload

{  
  eventType: "job_result",          // "app_access" | "job_start" | "job_result"  
  module: "atlas",                  // or "generators", "compendium"  
  jobId: "sector_survey_001",  
  summary: {  
    sectorSystems: 24,  
    worlds: 3,  
    starports: 2  
  },  
  message: "Sector survey complete.",  
  at: Date.now()  
}

3) Dashboard page logic (console + result tracker)

A) On load: initial boot

  • boot lines like:
    • [EHGC] Nexus Command Terminal online.
    • [EHGC] Dashboard console integrity check… OK

B) Listen for events from module pages

Poll localStorage for a single “bridge” key and append to console.

// dashboard-app.js  
const CONSOLE_KEY = "ehgc_bridge_event";  

function bootDashboardConsole() {  
  const c = document.getElementById("dashboardConsole");  
  c.innerHTML = "";  
  appendLine(c, "[EHGC] Dashboard console initializing…");  
  setTimeout(() => appendLine(c, "[EHGC] Routing tables armed."), 350);  
  setTimeout(() => appendLine(c, "[EHGC] Global telemetry armed (session-local)."), 750);  
}  

function appendLine(consoleEl, text) {  
  const div = document.createElement("div");  
  div.className = "console-line";  
  div.textContent = text;  
  consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
  consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
}  

function safeReadBridgeEvent() {  
  const raw = localStorage.getItem(CONSOLE_KEY);  
  if (!raw) return null;  
  localStorage.removeItem(CONSOLE_KEY);  
  return JSON.parse(raw);  
}  

function startDashboardBridgeListener() {  
  const consoleEl = document.getElementById("dashboardConsole");  

  setInterval(() => {  
    const evt = safeReadBridgeEvent();  
    if (!evt) return;  

    const timeTag = new Date(evt.at).toISOString().replace("T", " ").slice(0, 19);  

    if (evt.eventType === "app_access") {  
      appendLine(consoleEl, `[${timeTag}] APP_ACCESS ${evt.module}`);  
    } else if (evt.eventType === "job_start") {  
      appendLine(consoleEl, `[${timeTag}] JOB_START ${evt.module}:${evt.jobId}`);  
    } else if (evt.eventType === "job_result") {  
      // render a compact top-level summary line  
      const summary = evt.summary || {};  
      const pairs = Object.entries(summary).map(([k, v]) => `${k}=${v}`).join(" ");  
      appendLine(consoleEl, `[${timeTag}] JOB_RESULT ${evt.module}:${evt.jobId} ${pairs}`);  
    } else {  
      appendLine(consoleEl, `[${timeTag}] ${evt.module}: ${evt.message || evt.eventType}`);  
    }  

    // Optional counters:  
    // - increment active programs  
    // - decrement when job_result arrives  
  }, 250);  
}  

bootDashboardConsole();  
startDashboardBridgeListener();  

// Also: dashboard can log when user lands on each module via links/click handlers.

4) Generator module logic (local console + unique boot sequence)

Each module page uses a reusable “module console” helper, but with different boot sequences.

A) Local-only console that resets on each page load

Key points:

  • clear DOM console immediately on load
  • run timed boot steps
  • then append module activity

B) Unique boot sequence per module

Example for Atlas vs Generators:

// module-console.js  
export function createModuleConsole({  
  consoleId,  
  moduleName,  
  bootLines = []  
}) {  
  const el = document.getElementById(consoleId);  
  el.innerHTML = "";  

  const boot = () => {  
    append(`[${moduleName}] boot: phase 1/3…`);  
    step(0.35, () => append(`[${moduleName}] boot: phase 2/3…`));  
    step(0.75, () => append(`[${moduleName}] boot: phase 3/3… READY`));  
    // optionally append extra lines  
    // bootLines.forEach with per-step time offsets  
  };  

  const append = (text) => {  
    const div = document.createElement("div");  
    div.className = "console-line";  
    div.textContent = text;  
    el.appendChild(div);  
    el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;  
  };  

  const step = (seconds, fn) => setTimeout(fn, seconds * 1000);  

  boot();  
  return {  
    log: (text) => append(text),  
  };  
}

Then in atlas.html:

<script type="module">  
import { createModuleConsole } from "./module-console.js";  

const localConsole = createModuleConsole({  
  consoleId: "atlasConsole",  
  moduleName: "ATLAS",  
  bootLines: [  
    "binding star-map stream…",  
    "calibrating sector overlay grid…"  
  ]  
});  

localConsole.log("[ATLAS] operator session established.");  

function emitToDashboard(evt) {  
  localStorage.setItem("ehgc_bridge_event", JSON.stringify(evt));  
}  

emitToDashboard({  
  eventType: "app_access",  
  module: "atlas",  
  at: Date.now()  
});  

// When user starts sector survey:  
function onSectorSurvey() {  
  const jobId = "sector_survey_" + Date.now().toString(36);  

  localConsole.log(`[ATLAS] sector survey initiated (job ${jobId}).`);  
  emitToDashboard({  
    eventType: "job_start",  
    module: "atlas",  
    jobId,  
    at: Date.now()  
  });  

  // TODO: call your python/backend later; for now simulate results:  
  setTimeout(() => {  
    const summary = {  
      sectorSystems: 24,  
      worlds: 3,  
      starports: 2  
    };  

    localConsole.log(`[ATLAS] sector survey complete.`);  
    localConsole.log(`[ATLAS] ${Object.entries(summary).map(([k,v])=>k+"="+v).join(" ")}`);  

    emitToDashboard({  
      eventType: "job_result",  
      module: "atlas",  
      jobId,  
      message: "Sector survey complete.",  
      summary,  
      at: Date.now()  
    });  
  }, 1200);  
}  
</script>

5) Wiring “boot sequences” to your HTML templates

To keep this consistent:

  • dashboard HTML has id="dashboardConsole"
  • each module HTML has its own id="atlasConsole" / id="generatorsConsole" etc.
  • console markup remains the same; only moduleName + boot lines differ

6) Quick question (so I generate the exact code files next)

What do you want your module-to-dashboard bridge to key off of?

  1. Button-level actions (Generate clicks emit job_start/job_result)
  2. Auto-run when the page loads
  3. Both

Reply 1, 2, or 3, and tell me your generator modules list (e.g., Atlas, Generators, Compendium), and I’ll produce the exact index.html + atlas.html + generators.html wiring with the console boot sequences and event emission.

both
gpt-5.5

“Both” (Dashboard console and per-generator console on every generator page)

You want both consoles to exist simultaneously:

  1. Dashboard Console (global)
    • Logs app access and tracks results (e.g., Sector Survey → “Sector: Number of systems generated”, etc.).
  2. Generator Page Console (local)
    • Starts fresh on each page load (local-only history).
    • Has a unique boot sequence per generator module.

This fits Traveller 5’s framing of consoles as task enablers that provide feedback-governed direction, run routine processes, and require user intervention for exceptions.

Frontend architecture (recommended)

1) Dashboard (index.html)

  • Has a single console panel: #dashboardConsole
  • Responsible for:
    • APP_ACCESS module
    • JOB_START module:jobId
    • JOB_RESULT module:jobId with top-level summary fields

2) Generator pages (atlas.html, generators.html, compendium.html, etc.)

  • Each has:
    • A module main panel (work area)
    • A local console panel: e.g. #atlasConsole, #generatorsConsole
  • On load:
    • Clear local console history
    • Run that module’s boot sequence (timed lines + “READY”)

Communication bridge (frontend-only, no backend yet)

Because you’re using multi-page separate HTML files, the simplest “dashboard receives events” approach is:

  • Generator pages emit events into localStorage under one key (e.g. ehgc_bridge_event)
  • Dashboard polls and drains that key, then appends to the dashboard console

Event schema (what generators write)

{  
  eventType: "app_access" | "job_start" | "job_result",  
  module: "atlas" | "generators" | "compendium",  
  jobId: "sector_survey_..." ,  
  message: "optional human line",  
  summary: {  
    sectorSystems: 24,  
    worlds: 3,  
    starports: 2  
  },  
  at: Date.now()  
}

Dashboard behavior

  • On load: boot lines for the dashboard console
  • Every ~250ms:
    • read + remove ehgc_bridge_event
    • format based on eventType
    • append to #dashboardConsole

Traveller tie-in (why “both” makes sense)

  • Consoles provide routine governance and feedback-governed direction without needing constant user control.
  • Consoles cannot show initiative beyond routine operations; unusual/choice situations are exceptions that require the user’s instruction.

In your UI:

  • The local generator console handles routine progress (boot, status, step logs).
  • The dashboard console provides the higher-level session record (access + results), effectively serving as your session “monitor”.

Minimal next step: confirm your module list + boot style

To generate exact code, tell me:

  1. What are the generator pages you currently have (exact module names/files)? (e.g., atlas.html, generators.html, compendium.html)
  2. For “unique boot sequence”, do you want:
    • (A) different text only, or
    • (B) different timing/pattern too (e.g., 3 phases vs 5 phases)?

Once you answer, I’ll produce the concrete dashboard-app.js + per-module module-console.js + wiring for index.html and each generator HTML.

Hold up, I'm overloaded. What should my skelton html look like right now for atlas, compendium, generators. Logs and settings will have different html correct?
gpt-5.5

Yes—Atlas / Compendium / Generators should each have their own HTML layout, and Logs + Settings will be separate HTML pages.

Right now, based on your decisions:

  • Dashboard has the global Console.
  • Each generator module page (Atlas / Compendium / Generators) has:
    • its main-work UI
    • its own local Console (fresh on load) with a unique boot sequence
  • Logs and Settings are different modules, so they get their own HTML (with layouts appropriate to those tasks).

Below are starter “skeleton HTML” templates you can copy/paste.

1) Common structure (what every generator module page should include)

Each module page should have:

  • topbar (optional but recommended for consistency)
  • a grid with:
    • panel (main work)
    • panel console-panel (local console)
  • scripts:
    • shared UI helper (if you have it)
    • module boot / module wiring (module-specific)

2) atlas.html skeleton (with local console + unique boot)

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Atlas</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Atlas</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Main work -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Atlas Operations</h2>  
        <div class="panel-body">  
          <!-- TODO: Atlas widgets/forms -->  
          <button class="btn primary" id="atlasGenerate">⚡ GENERATE SECTOR SURVEY</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Local console (fresh on load) -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="atlasConsole">  
          <!-- Boot sequence lines injected by JS -->  
        </div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="atlasClearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="atlasRunSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Local Console • Atlas</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <!-- Keep these modular; names are examples -->  
  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    // Unique boot sequence for Atlas (local-only history)  
    const consoleEl = document.getElementById("atlasConsole");  
    consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  

    const bootLines = [  
      "[ATLAS] Initializing epistellar map stream…",  
      "[ATLAS] Calibrating orbital-zone resolver (Table 6)…",  
      "[ATLAS] Loading orbital contents resolver (Table 7)…",  
      "[ATLAS] Operator session ready."  
    ];  

    bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
      setTimeout(() => {  
        const div = document.createElement("div");  
        div.className = "console-line";  
        div.textContent = line;  
        consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
        consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
      }, 250 + i * 350);  
    });  

    // Example: generate button triggers local console + later sends to dashboard  
    document.getElementById("atlasGenerate").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      const jobId = "atlas_" + Date.now().toString(36);  

      // TODO: local console enqueue/complete; for now log lines:  
      appendLocal(consoleEl, `[ATLAS] JOB_START ${jobId} sector_survey…`);  

      setTimeout(() => {  
        // TODO: replace with backend results  
        appendLocal(consoleEl, `[ATLAS] JOB_RESULT ${jobId} Sector systems=24 Worlds=3 Starports=2`);  
      }, 1200);  
    });  

    function appendLocal(el, text) {  
      const div = document.createElement("div");  
      div.className = "console-line";  
      div.textContent = text;  
      el.appendChild(div);  
      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;  
    }  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

3) compendium.html skeleton (local console + unique boot)

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Galactic Compendium</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Galactic Compendium</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Main work -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Compendium Browser</h2>  
        <div class="panel-body">  
          <!-- TODO: search/filter + list of lore/entities -->  
          <label class="label">Query</label>  
          <input class="input" id="compendiumQuery" placeholder="e.g., sector name, species, system ID…" />  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:10px;">  
            <button class="btn primary" id="compendiumSearch">SEARCH</button>  
            <button class="btn" id="compendiumGenerate">⚡ GENERATE NEW LORE ENTRY</button>  
          </div>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Local console -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="compendiumConsole"></div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="compendiumClearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="compendiumRunSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Local Console • Compendium</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    const consoleEl = document.getElementById("compendiumConsole");  
    consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  

    // Unique boot sequence for Compendium  
    const bootLines = [  
      "[COMPENDIUM] Boot: index shards warming…",  
      "[COMPENDIUM] Boot: lore taxonomy loaded (cosmic/planetary/biological/narrative)…",  
      "[COMPENDIUM] Boot: entity serializer armed.",  
      "[COMPENDIUM] Operator session ready."  
    ];  

    bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
      setTimeout(() => {  
        const div = document.createElement("div");  
        div.className = "console-line";  
        div.textContent = line;  
        consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
        consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
      }, 250 + i * 350);  
    });  

    document.getElementById("compendiumSearch").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      const q = document.getElementById("compendiumQuery").value.trim() || "(empty)";  
      appendLocal(consoleEl, `[COMPENDIUM] SEARCH query="${q}"`);  
      // TODO: replace with backend  
      setTimeout(() => appendLocal(consoleEl, "[COMPENDIUM] SEARCH complete: 0 results (placeholder)."), 900);  
    });  

    document.getElementById("compendiumGenerate").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      const jobId = "comp_" + Date.now().toString(36);  
      appendLocal(consoleEl, `[COMPENDIUM] JOB_START ${jobId} generate_lore_entry…`);  
      setTimeout(() => {  
        appendLocal(consoleEl, `[COMPENDIUM] JOB_RESULT ${jobId} entry_type=history title="…"`);  
      }, 1100);  
    });  

    function appendLocal(el, text) {  
      const div = document.createElement("div");  
      div.className = "console-line";  
      div.textContent = text;  
      el.appendChild(div);  
      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;  
    }  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

4) generators.html skeleton (local console + unique boot)

<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Generators</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Generators</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Generator Modules</h2>  
        <div class="panel-body">  
          <!-- TODO: program selection list -->  
          <p class="muted">Select a generator and run it. Each run generates local console feedback.</p>  

          <div class="row">  
            <select class="input" id="generatorSelect">  
              <option value="sector_survey">Atlas • Sector Survey</option>  
              <option value="world_profile">Atlas • World Profile</option>  
              <option value="compendium_entry">Compendium • Lore Entry</option>  
            </select>  
            <button class="btn primary" id="runGenerator">⚡ GENERATE</button>  
          </div>  

          <div style="margin-top:12px;">  
            <label class="label">Seed (optional)</label>  
            <input class="input" id="generatorSeed" placeholder="e.g., 12345 or random" />  
          </div>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Local console -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="generatorsConsole"></div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="generatorsClearConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="generatorsRunSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Local Console • Generators</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    const consoleEl = document.getElementById("generatorsConsole");  
    consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  

    // Unique boot sequence for Generators  
    const bootLines = [  
      "[GEN] Boot: job scheduler online.",  
      "[GEN] Boot: generator registry indexed.",  
      "[GEN] Boot: output channel connected (local page console).",  
      "[GEN] Operator session ready."  
    ];  

    bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
      setTimeout(() => {  
        const div = document.createElement("div");  
        div.className = "console-line";  
        div.textContent = line;  
        consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
        consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
      }, 250 + i * 350);  
    });  

    document.getElementById("runGenerator").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      const job = document.getElementById("generatorSelect").value;  
      const seed = document.getElementById("generatorSeed").value.trim();  
      const jobId = "job_" + Date.now().toString(36);  

      appendLocal(consoleEl, `[GEN] JOB_START ${jobId} ${job} seed="${seed || "none"}" ...`);  

      // TODO: replace with backend  
      setTimeout(() => {  
        appendLocal(consoleEl, `[GEN] JOB_RESULT ${jobId} status=complete (placeholder).`);  
      }, 1000);  
    });  

    function appendLocal(el, text) {  
      const div = document.createElement("div");  
      div.className = "console-line";  
      div.textContent = text;  
      el.appendChild(div);  
      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;  
    }  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

5) Are Logs + Settings different HTML? (Yes)

You’ll likely create separate pages like:

  • log.html (maybe a read-only view of log entries)
  • settings.html (theme, seed defaults, backend endpoint config, etc.)

They should not necessarily mirror the “generator module main panel” content—they’ll have their own main panels, and depending on your UX preference they may or may not have consoles.

Quick question so I can finalize the exact templates

For Logs and Settings, do you want:

  1. No local console, just main content, OR
  2. A console too (fresh boot per page) like the generator modules?

Reply 1 or 2 and I’ll draft those two HTML skeletons too.

The log.html should be read-only view. I think it should be a console solely. Maybe with some filter options to filter out logs Settings.html I feel should have a console. logging changes and such
gpt-5.5

Confirmed: log.html read-only console, settings.html has a console

Based on your current UI concept (“Consoles provide feedback-governed direction; routine processes run without continual user intervention”):

  • log.html should be read-only and essentially just a console view.
  • settings.html should include a console (fresh boot per load) because you want to show logging/changes as the operator applies settings (and see confirmations/errors).

Also: this matches your current dashboard + module consoles model—log.html is for monitoring/reading, while settings.html is for changing configuration and receiving immediate feedback.

1) log.html skeleton (read-only console + optional filters)

Key points:

  • No “Generate” actions.
  • Console output is driven by stored log entries.
  • Add filters (module, eventType, time range) but keep it view-only.
<!-- log.html -->  
<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — System Log</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">System Log</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Filters (no actions that modify data) -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Filters</h2>  
        <div class="panel-body">  
          <div class="row">  
            <label class="label" for="filterModule">Module</label>  
            <select class="input" id="filterModule">  
              <option value="*">All</option>  
              <option value="atlas">Atlas</option>  
              <option value="generators">Generators</option>  
              <option value="compendium">Compendium</option>  
            </select>  
          </div>  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:10px;">  
            <label class="label" for="filterType">Event Type</label>  
            <select class="input" id="filterType">  
              <option value="*">All</option>  
              <option value="app_access">App Access</option>  
              <option value="job_start">Job Start</option>  
              <option value="job_result">Job Result</option>  
              <option value="log">Log</option>  
            </select>  
          </div>  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:10px;">  
            <label class="label" for="filterText">Text Contains</label>  
            <input class="input" id="filterText" placeholder="e.g., sector, Systems, READY…" />  
          </div>  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:12px;">  
            <button class="btn primary" id="applyLogFilters">Apply Filters</button>  
            <button class="btn" id="clearLogFilters">Clear</button>  
          </div>  

          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:12px; font-size:13px;">  
            View-only. Filtering changes what you see, not the stored records.  
          </p>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Read-only console-only view -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console (Read-Only)</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Visible Lines</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="visibleLogCount">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Total Stored</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="totalLogCount">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="logConsole" aria-readonly="true">  
          <!-- populated by JS -->  
        </div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn" id="exportLog">Export (JSON)</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Read-Only Log Console • EHGC</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    // Placeholder: wire to whichever storage you implement later (localStorage/SQLite/backend).  
    const logConsole = document.getElementById("logConsole");  

    function appendLine(text) {  
      const div = document.createElement("div");  
      div.className = "console-line";  
      div.textContent = text;  
      logConsole.appendChild(div);  
    }  

    function renderPlaceholder() {  
      logConsole.innerHTML = "";  
      appendLine("[EHGC] Log viewer ready (placeholder data).");  
      document.getElementById("visibleLogCount").textContent = "1";  
      document.getElementById("totalLogCount").textContent = "1";  
    }  

    document.getElementById("applyLogFilters").addEventListener("click", renderPlaceholder);  
    document.getElementById("clearLogFilters").addEventListener("click", renderPlaceholder);  
    renderPlaceholder();  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

Implementation note

When you later store logs in SQLite (or JSON files), log.html should read and render. Keep all “clear/delete” actions off this page to preserve read-only behavior.

2) settings.html skeleton (has a console; logs changes)

Key points:

  • Settings changes should append to the console: “SETTING UPDATED”, “VALIDATION ERROR”, etc.
  • This console can be “fresh per load” like your module consoles, since it’s about feedback for the current operator session.
<!-- settings.html -->  
<!doctype html>  
<html lang="en">  
<head>  
  <meta charset="utf-8" />  
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />  
  <title>EHGC — Settings</title>  
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />  
</head>  

<body>  
  <div class="app">  
    <header class="topbar">  
      <div class="brand">  
        <div class="brand-badge" aria-hidden="true"></div>  
        <div class="brand-text">  
          <div class="brand-title">EHGC</div>  
          <div class="brand-subtitle">Settings</div>  
        </div>  
      </div>  

      <div class="topbar-right">  
        <div class="status">  
          <span class="dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>  
          <span id="systemStatus">SYSTEM ONLINE</span>  
        </div>  
        <div class="clock">  
          <span id="utcClock">--:--:-- UTC</span>  
        </div>  
      </div>  
    </header>  

    <main class="grid">  
      <!-- Settings controls -->  
      <section class="panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Configuration</h2>  
        <div class="panel-body">  
          <div class="row">  
            <label class="label" for="defaultSeed">Default Seed</label>  
            <input class="input" id="defaultSeed" placeholder="random or 12345" />  
          </div>  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:10px;">  
            <label class="label" for="uiTheme">Theme</label>  
            <select class="input" id="uiTheme">  
              <option value="neon">Neon</option>  
              <option value="mono">Mono</option>  
              <option value="classic">Classic</option>  
            </select>  
          </div>  

          <div class="row" style="margin-top:12px;">  
            <button class="btn primary" id="saveSettings">Save Settings</button>  
            <button class="btn" id="resetSettings">Reset</button>  
          </div>  

          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:12px; font-size:13px;">  
            Changes here should generate console feedback (and ideally a dashboard-level log event later).  
          </p>  
        </div>  
      </section>  

      <!-- Settings console -->  
      <section class="panel console-panel">  
        <h2 class="panel-title">Console (Settings Feedback)</h2>  

        <div class="console-meta">  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Active Programs</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="activePrograms">0</div>  
          </div>  
          <div class="meta-item">  
            <div class="meta-label">Output Queue</div>  
            <div class="meta-value" id="outputQueue">0</div>  
          </div>  
        </div>  

        <div class="console" id="settingsConsole"></div>  

        <div class="console-actions">  
          <button class="btn primary" id="clearSettingsConsole">Clear Console</button>  
          <button class="btn" id="settingsSelfTest">Run Self-Test</button>  
        </div>  
      </section>  
    </main>  

    <footer class="footer">  
      <span class="footer-text">Operator Settings Console • EHGC</span>  
    </footer>  
  </div>  

  <script src="app-ui.js"></script>  
  <script>  
    const consoleEl = document.getElementById("settingsConsole");  
    consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  

    function appendLine(text) {  
      const div = document.createElement("div");  
      div.className = "console-line";  
      div.textContent = text;  
      consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
      consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
    }  

    // Unique boot sequence (fresh per page load)  
    const bootLines = [  
      "[SETTINGS] Boot: configuration bus connected…",  
      "[SETTINGS] Boot: validator modules loaded…",  
      "[SETTINGS] Boot: audit trail channel armed (session-local)…",  
      "[SETTINGS] Operator session ready."  
    ];  

    bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
      setTimeout(() => appendLine(line), 250 + i * 350);  
    });  

    // Placeholder persistence (later swap to SQLite/backend)  
    function saveSettings() {  
      const defaultSeed = document.getElementById("defaultSeed").value.trim();  
      const uiTheme = document.getElementById("uiTheme").value;  

      // TODO: validate; placeholder validation  
      if (defaultSeed === "0") {  
        appendLine("[SETTINGS] VALIDATION ERROR: seed value '0' is not allowed (example).");  
        return;  
      }  

      // store locally for now  
      localStorage.setItem("ehgc_defaultSeed", defaultSeed);  
      localStorage.setItem("ehgc_uiTheme", uiTheme);  

      appendLine(`[SETTINGS] SETTING UPDATED: defaultSeed="${defaultSeed || "(empty)"}"`);  
      appendLine(`[SETTINGS] SETTING UPDATED: uiTheme="${uiTheme}"`);  
      appendLine("[SETTINGS] Save complete: OK");  
      
      // Optional later: also emit to dashboard/global log  
      // localStorage.setItem("ehgc_bridge_event", JSON.stringify({ ... }));  
    }  

    document.getElementById("saveSettings").addEventListener("click", saveSettings);  

    document.getElementById("resetSettings").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      localStorage.removeItem("ehgc_defaultSeed");  
      localStorage.removeItem("ehgc_uiTheme");  
      appendLine("[SETTINGS] RESET requested.");  
      appendLine("[SETTINGS] RESET complete: defaults restored.");  
    });  

    document.getElementById("clearSettingsConsole").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  
      appendLine("[SETTINGS] Console cleared (session-local).");  
    });  

    document.getElementById("settingsSelfTest").addEventListener("click", () => {  
      appendLine("[SETTINGS] Running self-test…");  
      setTimeout(() => appendLine("[SETTINGS] Self-test complete: OK"), 800);  
    });  

    // Optional: populate fields from localStorage  
    document.getElementById("defaultSeed").value = localStorage.getItem("ehgc_defaultSeed") || "";  
    document.getElementById("uiTheme").value = localStorage.getItem("ehgc_uiTheme") || "neon";  
  </script>  
</body>  
</html>

One question (so I match your storage plan)

For log.html / “stored records”, do you want logs to be:

  1. Session-only (cleared on browser refresh), using in-memory or sessionStorage
  2. Persisted (kept across refresh), using localStorage now, then SQLite later

Reply 1 or 2, and I’ll align the skeleton rendering/export logic accordingly.

everything should be persisted. text file would be fine for these logs. Maybe setup a purge function after file reaches a fixed size
gpt-5.5

Persist everything (yes) + file-based logs (with purge by size)

Given your request, here’s a clean approach that matches your stack goals (Python backend, SQLite/JSON later) but starts with something immediately workable:

Recommended logging model

  • Persist all events (dashboard accesses, job starts/results, settings changes, generator actions).
  • Store logs as append-only lines in a text file (easy + fast).
  • Also keep a rolling/purge policy:
    • When the file exceeds a fixed size (e.g., 10MB / 50MB / configurable), purge or rotate.

This lets log.html be a read-only console backed by a real persisted history.

1) Log file format (text-friendly)

Use one JSON object per line (JSONL). That gives you:

  • easy append
  • easy filtering later
  • no complex parsing

Example line:

{"at":"2026-06-20T12:34:56.123Z","level":"INFO","scope":"atlas","eventType":"job_result","jobId":"atlas_k9x2","summary":{"sectorSystems":24,"worlds":3,"starports":2}}

This is still “text file”, but structured enough that log.html filters work reliably.

2) Purge / rotation strategies

You have 2 good options:

Option A (simplest): rotate then truncate

When size > limit:

  • rename current file to system_log.2026-06-20.jsonl
  • create a fresh system_log.jsonl
  • optionally keep last N rotated files

Option B: in-place purge (delete oldest lines)

When size > limit:

  • read from the beginning until you reach only the most recent X bytes/lines
  • write back only those
  • slower and more I/O-heavy, but keeps a single file

For a first build, choose Option A (rotate). It’s fast and robust.

3) What to persist (your consoles + tracking)

Persist events emitted from:

  • Dashboard console
    • app_access
    • job-level summaries (sectorSystems, etc.)
    • any “tracking results” you want shown later in log.html
  • Atlas / Generators / Compendium pages
    • local actions still show locally
    • also append to the global persisted log (so history exists)
  • Settings page
    • every setting update attempt + validation result
    • success/failure outcomes

This gives you a real “System Log” that log.html can render with filters.

(Your “local console is fresh on page load” still holds—local console is just UI history. The persisted log is global history.)

4) How log.html uses the persisted file

  • log.html is read-only and filterable.
  • Later you can:
    • fetch log contents from backend endpoint (recommended), or
    • in early stages, load the file via backend and stream filtered lines.

Filters you mentioned already map perfectly:

  • module/scope
  • eventType
  • text contains
  • time window

5) Minimal backend shape (Python)

When you’re ready to implement, I can provide the exact code, but conceptually:

  • log_event(event: dict):

    • add timestamp if missing
    • append JSONL line to logs/system_log.jsonl
    • check file size, rotate if needed

  • get_logs(filters) for log.html:

    • read file (or rotated files)
    • apply filter predicates
    • return sorted newest-first (or oldest-first)

This will work whether you keep it purely as files now, then later swap internals to SQLite.

6) One question to set your defaults

What fixed max size do you want before purge/rotation?

Pick one (or tell me your own):

  • 10MB
  • 50MB
  • 100MB

Also: how many rotated files should you keep (e.g., last 5)?

2.) Purge / rotation strategies option A 6.) 500MB and last 5
gpt-5.5

Purge / rotation strategies: Option A (rotate then truncate)

Goal

When your active log file grows beyond the fixed max size, you rotate it (keep the old contents as an archive file) and then start a new empty log file.

Option A behavior (what it means)

  • If system_log.jsonl size > 500 MB:
    1. Rename current file to something like system_log_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.jsonl
    2. Create a brand-new empty system_log.jsonl
    3. Keep only the last 5 rotated files; delete older archives

This avoids expensive “delete oldest lines” rewriting and is fast/robust for large logs.

6) Your requested defaults

  • Max size before rotation: 500 MB
  • Rotated archives retained: 5

So your retention policy becomes:

  • Always keep:
    • the current file: system_log.jsonl
    • plus the 5 most recent rotated archives
  • Delete the 6th+ oldest rotated archives.

Simple policy pseudocode

if size(system_log.jsonl) > 500MB:  
  rotate:  
    archive = "system_log_<timestamp>.jsonl"  
    rename current system_log.jsonl -> archive  
    create new empty system_log.jsonl  

  cleanup:  
    archives = sorted( system_log_*.jsonl by time, oldest->newest )  
    while count(archives) > 5:  
      delete oldest archive  
      remove it from archives list

One detail to confirm (small but important)

When you say “last 5”, do you mean:

  1. Last 5 rotated archives only (current file is not counted) ✅ (this is the usual interpretation), or
  2. Last 5 total files including the current active one?

If you confirm which one, I’ll lock the exact purge rule and naming convention for your backend.

Last 5 archives only
gpt-5.5

Confirmed interpretation: Last 5 rotated archives only

Yes: when you rotate, the retention rule should be:

  • Keep the active log file: system_log.jsonl
  • Keep the last 5 rotated archive files:
    • system_log_<timestamp>.jsonl (most recent 5)
  • Delete the 6th and older rotated archives

So “last 5” does not include the current active file.

This matches the normal Option A “rotate-then-prune-archives” behavior you described (rotate when system_log.jsonl exceeds 500MB, then prune archives down to 5).

I have 3 .js files: app-ui.js - this is currently blank main.js module.js what should each of these currently look like
gpt-5.5

Short answer

  • app-ui.js should hold shared UI helpers (append-to-console, date/clock widgets, common DOM wiring).
  • main.js should be your router / page bootstrap (detect which HTML page is loaded and initialize the correct module).
  • module.js should be the shared module framework (local program registry, job lifecycle, and logging hooks).

Below are clean working “skeleton” versions you can drop in right now. They assume your HTML pages already have elements like:

  • console divs with ids: atlasConsole, compendiumConsole, generatorsConsole, settingsConsole, logConsole
  • buttons: atlasGenerate, etc. (or adjust the ids).

app-ui.js (shared UI helpers) — should NOT be blank

// app-ui.js  
// Shared UI utilities used by main.js and module.js  

export function getEl(id) {  
  return document.getElementById(id);  
}  

export function setText(id, text) {  
  const el = getEl(id);  
  if (el) el.textContent = text;  
}  

export function appendConsoleLine(consoleEl, line, { prefixClass = "" } = {}) {  
  if (!consoleEl) return;  

  const div = document.createElement("div");  
  div.className = `console-line ${prefixClass}`.trim();  
  div.textContent = line;  
  consoleEl.appendChild(div);  
  consoleEl.scrollTop = consoleEl.scrollHeight;  
}  

export function clearConsole(consoleEl) {  
  if (!consoleEl) return;  
  consoleEl.innerHTML = "";  
}  

export function startUtcClock(el) {  
  if (!el) return;  

  function tick() {  
    const d = new Date();  
    const hh = String(d.getUTCHours()).padStart(2, "0");  
    const mm = String(d.getUTCMinutes()).padStart(2, "0");  
    el.textContent = `${hh}:${mm} UTC`;  
  }  

  tick();  
  return setInterval(tick, 1000 * 15);  
}  

// Small helper to run boot lines with delays  
export function bootConsole(consoleEl, bootLines, { startMs = 250, stepMs = 350 } = {}) {  
  if (!consoleEl) return;  

  bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
    setTimeout(() => appendConsoleLine(consoleEl, line), startMs + i * stepMs);  
  });  
}

module.js (module/job framework + local console wiring)

// module.js  
// Shared job/module framework for Atlas / Generators / Compendium etc.  

import { appendConsoleLine, clearConsole } from "./app-ui.js";  

export function createLocalConsole(consoleEl, meta = {}) {  
  const state = {  
    activePrograms: 0,  
    outputQueue: 0,  
    meta  
  };  

  return {  
    state,  
    setActivePrograms(n) {  
      const el = meta.activeProgramsEl;  
      if (el) el.textContent = String(n);  
      state.activePrograms = n;  
    },  
    setOutputQueue(n) {  
      const el = meta.outputQueueEl;  
      if (el) el.textContent = String(n);  
      state.outputQueue = n;  
    },  
    log(line) {  
      appendConsoleLine(consoleEl, line);  
    },  
    clear() {  
      clearConsole(consoleEl);  
      // reset counters  
      this.setActivePrograms(0);  
      this.setOutputQueue(0);  
    }  
  };  
}  

export function bootModuleConsole(localConsole, bootLines) {  
  localConsole.clear();  
  bootLines.forEach((line, i) => {  
    setTimeout(() => localConsole.log(line), 250 + i * 350);  
  });  
}  

// Very small placeholder “job runner”.  
// Later you’ll swap the body to call your Python backend.  
export function runJob({ localConsole, jobId, jobName, seed = null, doWork }) {  
  localConsole.setOutputQueue(localConsole.state.outputQueue + 1);  
  localConsole.setActivePrograms(localConsole.state.activePrograms + 1);  

  localConsole.log(`[${jobName.toUpperCase()}] JOB_START ${jobId} seed="${seed ?? "none"}" ...`);  

  const workPromise = doWork ? doWork() : Promise.resolve(null);  

  return Promise.resolve(workPromise).then((result) => {  
    const summary = result && result.summary ? ` ${result.summary}` : "";  
    localConsole.log(  
      `[${jobName.toUpperCase()}] JOB_RESULT ${jobId} status=complete${summary}`  
    );  
  }).catch((err) => {  
    localConsole.log(  
      `[${jobName.toUpperCase()}] JOB_RESULT ${jobId} status=error error="${String(err)}"`  
    );  
  }).finally(() => {  
    localConsole.setOutputQueue(Math.max(0, localConsole.state.outputQueue - 1));  
    localConsole.setActivePrograms(Math.max(0, localConsole.state.activePrograms - 1));  
  });  
}

main.js (router + page bootstrapping) — should initialize correct page

This should be the entrypoint loaded by your HTML pages. It detects page type and wires buttons.

Note: Your HTML currently loads app-ui.js only. If you want main.js used, you must include it in HTML too:

<script type="module" src="main.js"></script>

(or adjust to your current setup).

Skeleton main.js

// main.js  
// Detect which page is loaded, then initialize appropriate UI wiring.  

import { getEl, startUtcClock, bootConsole } from "./app-ui.js";  
import { createLocalConsole, bootModuleConsole, runJob } from "./module.js";  

// Helper: which page are we on?  
function inferPage() {  
  const title = document.title.toLowerCase();  

  // Best: use a reliable marker if you add one later:  
  // <body data-page="atlas">  
  // For now: infer from title.  

  if (title.includes("atlas")) return "atlas";  
  if (title.includes("compendium")) return "compendium";  
  if (title.includes("generators")) return "generators";  
  if (title.includes("system log") || title.includes("log")) return "log";  
  if (title.includes("settings")) return "settings";  
  return "unknown";  
}  

function initGeneratorsPage() {  
  const consoleEl = getEl("generatorsConsole");  
  const local = createLocalConsole(consoleEl, {  
    activeProgramsEl: getEl("activePrograms"),  
    outputQueueEl: getEl("outputQueue")  
  });  

  bootModuleConsole(local, [  
    "[GEN] Boot: job scheduler online.",  
    "[GEN] Boot: generator registry indexed.",  
    "[GEN] Boot: output channel connected (local page console).",  
    "[GEN] Operator session ready."
